channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: US Military Shot Down Iranian Drones Launched Towards Strait Of Hormuz

media:content.media:description: "The attack drones posed an immediate threat to regional maritime traffic," US Central Command said.

item.description: Iran has launched multiple drones towards the Strait of Hormuz, CNN reported on Friday, citing a US official who also said at least four drones were shot down by US aircraft.
item.content:encoded: Iran has launched multiple drones towards the Strait of Hormuz, CNN reported on Friday, citing a US official who also said at least four drones were shot down by US aircraft.

item.title: US-Iran Live Updates: US Says It Carried Out Strikes On Iranian Radar Sites

media:content.media:description: US-Iran War Live: The US said that it had "successfully defeated" a series of Iranian missile attacks.

item.description: US-Iran War Live: Iran fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain hours after US conducted strikes on Iran's Qeshm Island amid ongoing peace talks between Washington and Tehran.
item.content:encoded: US-Iran War Live: Iran fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain hours after US conducted strikes on Iran's Qeshm Island amid ongoing peace talks between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: US Court Strikes Down Trump Policies Targeting Immigrants From 39 Countries

media:content.media:description: A US federal judge has blocked Donald Trump's strict immigration and travel policies.

item.description: A federal judge on Friday ruled that US President Donald Trump's administration had adopted a series of unlawful policies that have barred people from 39 countries from receiving decisions on applications for asylum, work permits, green cards and citizenship.
item.content:encoded: A federal judge on Friday ruled that US President Donald Trump's administration had adopted a series of unlawful policies that have barred people from 39 countries from receiving decisions on applications for asylum, work permits, green cards and citizenship.

item.title: "Righteous Anger": JD Vance Blames EU Migration Policy For UK Student's Murder

media:content.media:description: JD Vance said that UK student's murder is as tragic as it is enraging.

item.description: JD Vance's comments on the case of 18-year-old Henry Nowak sparked a swift denunciation from British Prime Minister Keir Starmer's office, which rejected attempts to "interfere in our democracy."
item.content:encoded: JD Vance's comments on the case of 18-year-old Henry Nowak sparked a swift denunciation from British Prime Minister Keir Starmer's office, which rejected attempts to "interfere in our democracy."

item.title: High-Risk Iran Mission For Uranium Trump Considered, Then Walked Away From

media:content.media:description: Trump suggested the United States still has the capability to carry out such an operation

item.description: Speaking from the Oval Office on Thursday, Trump said the operation was deemed too risky and would have required a major military effort. According to him, the mission would have taken at least two weeks to complete and involved transporting large amounts of equipment into the country.
item.content:encoded: Speaking from the Oval Office on Thursday, Trump said the operation was deemed too risky and would have required a major military effort. According to him, the mission would have taken at least two weeks to complete and involved transporting large amounts of equipment into the country.

item.title: Inside Trump's Blitzy Washington Revamp: Ballroom, Gardens And More

media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is pursuing an ambitious revamp of Washington.

item.description: Trump's building blitz is among the most dramatic by a US president since Theodore Roosevelt championed a structural overhaul of the National Mall in the early 1900s.
item.content:encoded: Trump's building blitz is among the most dramatic by a US president since Theodore Roosevelt championed a structural overhaul of the National Mall in the early 1900s.

item.title: Russia To Launch Own Smaller Version Of Elon Musk's Starlink In 2027

media:content.media:description: Starlink is banned in Russia.

item.description: Ukraine said in January that Russia was using Starlink terminals to guide its drones into Ukrainian territory.
item.content:encoded: Ukraine said in January that Russia was using Starlink terminals to guide its drones into Ukrainian territory.

item.title: "No Point": Putin Turns Down Zelensky's Rare Appeal To Meet Him

media:content.media:description: Zelensky says a meeting between the two men is needed to hash out the key issues of a peace deal.

item.description: Zelensky had on Thursday issued a rare direct appeal to the Russian leader and proposed a meeting between the two of them.
item.content:encoded: Zelensky had on Thursday issued a rare direct appeal to the Russian leader and proposed a meeting between the two of them.

item.title: Trump Wanted Green Card Applicants To Go Home. Then Came A Warning From CEOs

media:content.media:description: The tech industry and US Chamber of Commerce raised issues with the White House

item.description: On May 22, the US Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) announced that applicants for permanent status should apply from their home countries "except in extraordinary circumstances".
item.content:encoded: On May 22, the US Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) announced that applicants for permanent status should apply from their home countries "except in extraordinary circumstances".

item.title: First "Universal Vaccine" Entirely Designed By AI Tested On Humans

media:content.media:description: Nearly 40 people participated in the trial between late 2021 and 2023. (Representational)

item.description: The experimental jab is intended to be a "universal vaccine" which protects people against a range of viruses that have previously sparked deadly outbreaks including SARS, MERS and Covid-19.
item.content:encoded: The experimental jab is intended to be a "universal vaccine" which protects people against a range of viruses that have previously sparked deadly outbreaks including SARS, MERS and Covid-19.

item.title: WHO Announces $518-Million Plan To Fight Ebola Outbreak In Africa

media:content.media:description: The strategy aims to help the Democratic Republic of Congo and neighbouring Uganda.

item.description: So far there have been 381 confirmed cases in Congo and 62 confirmed deaths, according to Africa CDC. Uganda has recorded 19 cases and two deaths.
item.content:encoded: So far there have been 381 confirmed cases in Congo and 62 confirmed deaths, according to Africa CDC. Uganda has recorded 19 cases and two deaths.

item.title: "Be Realistic": Iran Dismisses Idea Of Trump Meeting Mojtaba Khamenei

media:content.media:description: Trump had earlier expressed that he would like to meet Mojtaba Khamenei.

item.description: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei was appointed Iran's supreme leader in March after the death of his father and predecessor Ali Khamenei in US-Israeli strikes on February 28.
item.content:encoded: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei was appointed Iran's supreme leader in March after the death of his father and predecessor Ali Khamenei in US-Israeli strikes on February 28.

item.title: Iran Claims Fired Missiles On 2 US Warships, CENTCOM Says "Did Not"

media:content.media:description: The US military's Central Command was quick to deny that the incident had taken place.

item.description: It is the latest episode to shake a ceasefire announced on April 8 that has largely halted hostilities between Iran and the United States.
item.content:encoded: It is the latest episode to shake a ceasefire announced on April 8 that has largely halted hostilities between Iran and the United States.

item.title: Unseen Photos Surface 37 Years After China's Tiananmen Square Protests

media:content.media:description: The photographs were taken by a Chinese state media photographer.

item.description: Thousands of people are estimated to have been killed on June 4, 37 years ago.
item.content:encoded: Thousands of people are estimated to have been killed on June 4, 37 years ago.

item.title: Video: Russia Amusement Park Names Ride After Nuke-Ready Missile 'Oreshnik'

media:content.media:description: The "Oreshnik" ride, named after a nuclear-capable hypersonic missile, at an amusement park in Russia.

item.description: The Oreshnik, which Russia first fired against Ukraine in 2024, and most recently last month, has a range of over 5,000 km (3,100 miles).
item.content:encoded: The Oreshnik, which Russia first fired against Ukraine in 2024, and most recently last month, has a range of over 5,000 km (3,100 miles).

item.title: 'Extremely Intelligent' Bear Attacks 4, Escapes Factory Through Locked Window In Japan

media:content.media:description: Fukushima City officials said the bear had been hit with a tranquilliser dart.

item.description: Authorities in Fukushima continue searching for a bear that injured four people before escaping an electronics factory by opening a locked window.
item.content:encoded: Authorities in Fukushima continue searching for a bear that injured four people before escaping an electronics factory by opening a locked window.

item.title: Watch: US Forces Board Oil Tanker Carrying Iranian Crude In Indian Ocean

media:content.media:description: US forces boarded an oil tanker in the Indian Ocean overnight.

item.description: The Davina was sanctioned in 2024 by the US Treasury, which said it had delivered oil from Iran to China.
item.content:encoded: The Davina was sanctioned in 2024 by the US Treasury, which said it had delivered oil from Iran to China.

item.title: Astronauts On International Space Station In Evacuation Mode As Russians Fix Air Leak

media:content.media:description: Four astronauts of NASA's Crew-12 mission on the station.

item.description: The air leaks have been relatively minor in recent months but escalated on Monday from a pound of air per day to two pounds.
item.content:encoded: The air leaks have been relatively minor in recent months but escalated on Monday from a pound of air per day to two pounds.

item.title: Israel Secretly Sent "Elite" Troops To Azerbaijan For Ops In Iran War: Report

media:content.media:description: The forces were initially planned to be rescue teams, but then they expanded operations

item.description: The quiet deployment in Azerbaijan is only one of the few military positions in the Middle East that gave Israel unprecedented access to Iran. The posting only shows how big of a role the neighbouring countries played to assist in operations against Tehran.
item.content:encoded: The quiet deployment in Azerbaijan is only one of the few military positions in the Middle East that gave Israel unprecedented access to Iran. The posting only shows how big of a role the neighbouring countries played to assist in operations against Tehran.

item.title: How Ukraine's Satellite Intelligence Move Is Saving Money, Time, Lives

media:content.media:description: The visuals has reduced the time to locate and strike Russian assets by almost 90 per cent.

item.description: The advantages of using this technology and imagery provided by Colorado-based Vantor extend beyond the precision of Ukraine's attacks.
item.content:encoded: The advantages of using this technology and imagery provided by Colorado-based Vantor extend beyond the precision of Ukraine's attacks.
